title: Performing CUT&RUN on adherent cells in a multi-well cell culture plate
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1emln","text":"This protocol describes a variant of the standard CUT\u0026RUN procedure (Skene et al. eLife 2017; Skene et al. Nature Protocols 2018; Meers et al. eLife 2019).  This streamlined protocol is immediately applicable to adherent cells maintained in a multi-well cell culture plate.  Trypsinising cultured cells to harvest and attaching them onto Concanavalin A-beads are not required.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":82,"length":5},{"style":"italic","offset":107,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":143,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"21f7u","text":"In the manuscript \"CUT\u0026RUN reveals unique positioning of pre-initiated RNA polymerase II in the steady state of transcription\", this protocol was performed on the human lung carcinoma cell line A549 cultured in a 24-well polystyrene plate (https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.07.07.191478v1).  The protocol is expected to work for other adherent cells that are firmly attached to the plate.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":18,"length":108}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":240,"length":59}],"data":[]},{"key":"f3qu4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c4gnd","text":"Antibodies used in the above manuscript:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78hp7","text":"Millipore, 05-623 (mouse anti-RNA polymerase II, clone CTD4H8; 0.75 μg per well)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"165u9","text":"Abcam, ab5095 (rabbit anti-RNA polymerase II (phospho S2), polyclonal; 0.75 μg per well)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cdfdi","text":"Abcam, ab5131 (rabbit anti-RNA polymerase II (phospho S5), polyclonal; 0.675 μg per well)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3hvq5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ehiqa","text":"Recombinant pAG-MNase:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5cmea","text":"Purified recombinant pAG-MNase from two commercial venders were tested, of which only one from Cell Signaling (Product# 40366) worked well with this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"81E37AA7C59E11EA82110A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.07.07.191478v1"}}}}

steps: Seed adherent cells in a standard 24-well cell culture plate Seed the cells at an appropriate density so that the cells are 70 to 90% confluent when performing CUT&RUN.
Perform CUT&RUN Before cell permeabilisation (step 2.2), Remove the cell culture medium Wash the cells with PBS (Optional) Cells can be fixed prior to CUT&RUN (step 2.1)

(Optional) Cells can be fixed by one of the followings: (1) Fix intact cells Add 500 μl of 1.5% formaldehyde (diluted in PBS) to a well and incubate for 10 min Wash cells ×3 with PBS go to step 2.2 (Permeabilisation) (2) Fix extracted cells Add 500 μl Perm buffer to a well and incubate for 5 min Wash cells ×2 with PBS Add 500 μl of 1.5% formaldehyde (diluted in PBS) to a well and incubate for 10 min Wash cells ×3 with PBS go to step 2.2 (Permeabilisation)
Permeabilisation Dispense 500 μl Perm buffer per well and incubate on the bench (i.e. at room temperature) for 15 min Remove the buffer and wash the cells once with Perm buffer

Antibody incubation Prepare antibody dilution* in Perm buffer (150 μl per well) Dispense 150 μl of the diluted antibody to a well and incubate on the bench for an hour Remove the antibody solution from the well and wash the cells ×2 with Perm buffer *1:100 dilution for antibodies of 0.5 μg/μl stock concentration and 1:200 for antibodies of 1 μg/μl ( https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.07.07.191478v1 ).

pAG-MNase incubation Prepare pAG-MNase dilution (Cell Signaling, 40366; 1:33 volume) in 150 μl Perm buffer per well Dispense 150 μl of the diluted pAG-MNase into a well and incubate on the bench for an hour Remove the pAG-MNase solution from the well and wash the cells ×2 with Perm buffer* *At the second wash, place the cell culture plate on ice-cold water to get ready for the chromatin digestion (step 2.5).

Chromatin digestion Prepare Perm buffer containing 5 mM CaCl 2 (add 1/20 volume of 100 mM CaCl 2 to Perm buffer) and chill it on ice Make sure that the cells are chilled on ice-cold water Dispense 150 μl cold Perm buffer containing 5 mM CaCl 2 and incubate the plate on ice-cold water for 30 min

Fragment release Add 50 μl 4× STOP solution to a well (50 μl per well) Gently rock the plate to mix the solution Place the cell culture plate in an incubator at 37ºC for 30 min Collect the supernatant (∼200 μl)

Fragment purification If the cells were not fixed, purify the DNA fragment using DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit (Qiagen, 69504). If fixed (step 2.1), add 10 μl of 20% SDS to the supernatant (1% in final concentration) and incubate overnight at 65ºC. Then add Proteinase K and incubate at 56ºC for an hour. Purify the DNA with QIAquick PCR Purification Kit (Qiagen, 28104).
creator: Michi Miura
